title: Correlated Subqueries
content:
### Correlated Subqueries

A correlated subquery references columns from the outer query. It is evaluated once for each row processed by the outer query.

---

### How Correlated Subqueries Work

Unlike regular subqueries which run once, correlated subqueries run repeatedly:

```sql
-- For each employee, find their department's average salary
SELECT e.name, e.salary,
       (SELECT AVG(salary)
        FROM employees
        WHERE department = e.department) AS dept_avg
FROM employees e;

-- The subquery runs once per employee row
```

### Correlated Subquery in WHERE

```sql
-- Find employees earning more than their department average
SELECT e.name, e.salary, e.department
FROM employees e
WHERE e.salary > (
    SELECT AVG(salary)
    FROM employees
    WHERE department = e.department
);
```

### Correlated vs Non-Correlated

| Aspect | Non-Correlated | Correlated |
|--------|---------------|------------|
| Evaluation | Once | Once per outer row |
| Performance | Fast | Slower on large tables |
| Dependency | Independent | References outer query |
| Complexity | Simple | More complex |

### EXISTS with Correlated Subquery

```sql
-- Find customers who have placed at least one order
SELECT c.name
FROM customers c
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM orders o
    WHERE o.customer_id = c.id
);
```

> **Key Point:** Correlated subqueries can be slow because they run once per row of the outer query. Consider using JOIN or window functions as performance alternatives.
codeExample:
SELECT p.product_name, p.price
FROM products p
WHERE p.price > (
    SELECT AVG(price)
    FROM products
    WHERE category = p.category
);

quiz.question: How many times does a correlated subquery execute?
options: Once per row of the outer query

quiz.question: What makes a subquery correlated?
options: It references columns from the outer query
It has a GROUP BY clause
